findings.suggestion: Split into separate modules: api-key-manager.js, model-config.js, billing-config.js
Consider extracting: checksum logic, file I/O operations, period reset logic into separate modules
Extract agent prompt definitions to separate file (agent-prompts.js)
Extract WORKER_PROMPTS and NEXT_STEPS to separate constants file

findings.issue: Duplicated spawner logic between CLI and MCP server
findings.suggestion: Consider extracting shared agent spawning logic to @cervellaswarm/core package

findings.issue: 336 console.log statements found in CLI package
findings.suggestion: These are intentional for CLI output - OK. Consider adding logger abstraction for future if needed.

findings.issue: 2 TODO comments found for future features
findings.suggestion: Create GitHub issues for: SNCP resources (L323), MCP prompts (L332)

strengths: Excellent TypeScript usage in MCP server
Good separation of concerns (commands, agents, config, display)
Comprehensive error handling with custom CervellaError
Atomic file writes with backup in usage tracker
weaknesses: Some code duplication between CLI and MCP spawners
No shared core library for common logic

immediate: Split config/manager.js into 3 modules (api-key, model, billing)
Create GitHub issues for TODOs in mcp-server
short_term: Extract agent prompts to separate constants files
long_term: Refactor spawner logic to eliminate duplication
Add logger abstraction layer for better testability

notes: Overall codebase is in excellent shape for v2.0.0-beta.1. No critical issues. Main concern is config/manager.js size. Code is clean, well-documented, and follows best practices.
